response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack software developer with a strong testing culture and some distributed systems exposure, but he fails to meet the role's most critical minimum qualification by a wide margin. The position requires 8 years of C++ programming experience; the resume shows only incidental C++ use — roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — with C++ absent from his listed skills entirely. His technical identity is firmly rooted in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and application development. There is no demonstrated track record of cloud compute platform engineering, large-scale infrastructure ownership, or technical leadership of platform or DevOps teams, all of which are central to this role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship ethos with deep TDD/XP practice — has built and open-sourced testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and evangelized testing practices through workshops and dojos
Demonstrated exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency design), showing some systems-level thinking
Staff-level seniority with cross-team impact: mentoring, public speaking, open-source platform ownership (GISCollective on Kubernetes), and DevOps infrastructure setup at Altom
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months incidental use) versus the 8-year hard minimum requirement — C++ is not listed anywhere in his skills section, making this a critical disqualifier
No evidence of cloud compute platform engineering, hardware architecture, or large-scale infrastructure ownership at the depth or tenure this role demands

No demonstrated technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineering teams; staff roles appear individual-contributor-heavy without the platform strategy or cross-team architecture ownership the position requires
